case: Pigors v. Watson — 14th Circuit Court, Case No. 2024-001507-DC

first_eval.result: CLEAN — No safety issues, ADHD controlled

second_eval.new_clinical_data: NONE — only FOC ex parte contact

bias_indicators.description: First evaluation (9/4/2025): CLEAN result — no safety issues found, ADHD controlled with medication. Second evaluation (9/11/2025): Rule-out "delusional disorder" — only 7 calendar days later with NO new clinical evidence. This 180-degree reversal without new data strongly suggests external influence.
bias_indicators.legal_significance: APA Ethics Code 9.01(a) requires sufficient data for opinions

bias_indicators.description: Pamela Rusco (FOC) directly called the HealthWest clinician AND emailed a subpoena between the two evaluations. This constitutes ex parte communication with the evaluator. Found 108 evidence quotes referencing Rusco.
bias_indicators.legal_significance: MCR 2.410; MRPC 3.5 — prohibited ex parte contact with experts

bias_indicators.description: The "delusional disorder" rule-out appears based on Andrew's assertion that "this whole thing is illegal" — which is a protected legal opinion, not a psychiatric symptom. Treating legal advocacy as psychopathology is a well-documented form of institutional abuse.
bias_indicators.legal_significance: 1st Amendment — protected speech; Petition Clause — right to seek redress; APA Ethics 2.04 — opinions must be based on scientific evidence

bias_indicators.description: Between 9/4 and 9/11, no new psychological testing was administered, no new collateral contacts were documented, and no behavioral observations changed. The only new variable was the FOC's ex parte contact with the clinician.
bias_indicators.legal_significance: Daubert/FRE 702 — expert opinions require reliable methodology

bias_indicators.description: Pattern consistent with "evaluator capture" where a court-aligned party (FOC Rusco) influences the nominally independent evaluator through selective information sharing, creating a feedback loop that confirms the court's predetermined narrative.
bias_indicators.legal_significance: MCL 722.27b — evaluation must be independent; Daubert v. Merrell Dow — reliability standard

ex_parte_chain.outcome: No safety issues. ADHD controlled. No delusional features.
Rusco directly called clinician and emailed subpoena. This contact occurred WITHOUT notice to Andrew (ex parte).
Rule-out "delusional disorder" diagnosis. Basis: Andrew's statement "this whole thing is illegal." 180-degree reversal from 7 days prior with no new clinical data.

ex_parte_chain.event: Court uses evaluation to restrict parenting time
ex_parte_chain.outcome: The tainted evaluation becomes the basis for continued restriction of Andrew's parenting time — converting an ex parte-influenced evaluation into a custody weapon.
